content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


June 24, 1992


Carl G. Olson
Elsie Olson & Associates
Campaign Accounting
18309 San Fernando Mission Blvd.
Northridge, CA 91326

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-92-296

Dear Mr. Olson:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")1/ which regulate the appropriate use of campaign funds. Because your request is a general inquiry, we treat it as one for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

QUESTION

May surplus campaign funds be donated to the Senate Republican Caucus?

CONCLUSION

Once campaign funds become surplus funds, the Act limits their use. Permissible use of surplus funds raised after January 1, 1989, does not include funding committees which are in support of or opposition to a candidate for state or local elective office.


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91915. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

C. G. Olson
Our File No. I-92-296
Page 2

ANALYSIS

Campaign funds under the control of a candidate or officeholder become surplus funds when the officeholder leaves office or at the end of the post-election reporting period following the defeat of the candidate. (Section 85807.) Once campaign funds become surplus, their use is limited. Under Section 85807, surplus funds can be used only for the following purposes:

(a) The payment of outstanding campaign debts or elected officer's expenses.

(b) The pro rata repayment of contributions.

(c) Donations to any bona fide charitable, educational, civic, religious, or similar tax-exempt, nonprofit organization, where no substantial part of the proceeds will have a material financial effect on the former candidate or elected officer, any member of his or her immediate family, or his or her campaign treasurer.

(d) Contributions to a political party or committee so long as the funds are not used to make contributions in support of or opposition to a candidate for elective office.

(e) Contributions to support or oppose any candidate for federal office, any candidate for elective office in a state other than California, or any ballot measure.

You have also asked whether the Senate Republican Caucus is still in existence. To determine this committee's status, you should contact the Secretary of State's office at (916) 322-4880.

If you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature]

By: Linda Moureaux
Political Reform Consultant
